title: Gurdwara Sri Ber Sahib
tagline: Gurdwara Sri Ber Sahib — heritage city of Punjab

overview.short: Gurdwara Sri Ber Sahib is a gurdwara in the city of Sultanpur Lodhi, Kapurthala district, Punjab, India, situated on the banks of Kali Bein rivulet.
overview.description: Gurdwara Sri Ber Sahib is a gurdwara in the city of Sultanpur Lodhi, Kapurthala district, Punjab, India, situated on the banks of Kali Bein rivulet. It commemorates the place where Guru Nanak Dev, the first Sikh guru, had a mystical experience leading to his founding of the Sikh religion.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Shahjahanpur, Sultanpur Lodhi
topPlaces.description: Shahjahanpur is a village in Sultanpur Lodhi tehsil in Kapurthala district of Punjab, India. It is located 8 kilometres (5.0 mi) from the city of Sultanpur Lodhi, 33 kilometres (21 mi) away from district headquarter Kapurthala.

topPlaces.name: Alluwal
topPlaces.description: Alluwal is a village in Sultanpur Lodhi tehsil in Kapurthala district of Punjab, India. It is located 8 kilometres (5.0 mi) from the city of Sultanpur Lodhi, 35 kilometres (22 mi) away from district headquarter Kapurthala.

topPlaces.name: Alam Khanwala
topPlaces.description: Alam Khanwala is a village in Sultanpur Lodhi tehsil in Kapurthala district of Punjab, India. It is located 17 kilometres (11 mi) from the city of Sultanpur Lodhi, 43 kilometres (27 mi) away from district headquarter Kapurthala.

topPlaces.name: Akalpur, Sultanpur Lodhi
topPlaces.description: Akalpur is a village in Sultanpur Lodhi tehsil in Kapurthala district of Punjab, India. It is located 20 kilometres (12 mi) from the city of Sultanpur Lodhi, 35 kilometres (22 mi) away from district headquarter Kapurthala.

topPlaces.name: Sultanpur Lodhi
topPlaces.description: Sultanpur Lodhi is a city and a Municipal Council, 17 miles (27 km) from Kapurthala city in the Kapurthala district in the Indian state of Punjab. The town is named after its founder, Bahlul Khan Lodi, the future Sultan of Delhi who renamed the town in 1443 C.E. during his time as governor of Punjab, and was also mentioned in the Ain-e-Akbari.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gurdwara Sri Ber Sahib?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gurdwara Sri Ber Sahib is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gurdwara Sri Ber Sahib?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport, Amritsar (66 km) and the nearest railway station is Amritsar Junction (56 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gurdwara Sri Ber Sahib?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gurdwara Sri Ber Sahib at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gurdwara Sri Ber Sahib?
faq.a: Stays in Gurdwara Sri Ber Sahib start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gurdwara Sri Ber Sahib?
faq.a: The top attractions are Shahjahanpur, Sultanpur Lodhi, Alluwal, Alam Khanwala, Akalpur, Sultanpur Lodhi, Sultanpur Lodhi.
